Home › Calculators › Kaffara & Fidya Calculator
⚖️

Kaffara & Fidya Calculator

Calculate the required expiation and its approximate cost for eight types of kaffara and fidya

🍽️
Two meals or one mudd of food

Oath Expiation (Kaffarat al-Yamin)

Whoever swears an oath and breaks it must offer expiation.

Shari'a Priority Order
1
Feed ten poor people
—
or
2
Clothe ten poor people
—
or
3
Free a slave
Freeing a slave is no longer practically applicable in this era
If unable
4
Fast three days
—
The first three options are interchangeable (choice). If unable to do any, then fast three days.

Zihar Expiation

Whoever makes zihar (declares his wife as unlawful like his mother's back) and wishes to return to her must offer expiation before intimacy.

Shari'a Priority Order
1
Free a slave
Freeing a slave is no longer practically applicable in this era
If unable
2
Fast two consecutive months (60 days)
—
If unable
3
Feed sixty poor people
—

Ramadan Violation Expiation

Whoever deliberately has intercourse during a Ramadan fasting day must offer expiation and make up that day.

Shari'a Priority Order
1
Free a slave
Freeing a slave is no longer practically applicable in this era
If unable
2
Fast two consecutive months (60 days)
—
If unable
3
Feed sixty poor people
—

Accidental Killing Expiation

Whoever accidentally kills a believing soul must offer expiation in addition to the blood money (diyah).

Shari'a Priority Order
1
Free a believing slave
Freeing a slave is no longer practically applicable in this era
If unable
2
Fast two consecutive months (60 days)
—
There is no feeding option for accidental killing expiation

Fasting Ransom (Fidyat al-Siyam)

For those unable to fast due to old age or chronic incurable illness.

1
Feed one poor person for each day
Total Cost
—

Vow Expiation (Kaffarat al-Nadhr)

Whoever makes a vow and is unable to fulfill it must offer expiation like that of a broken oath.

Same as oath expiation — feed or clothe ten poor people, or free a slave. If unable, fast three days.
Shari'a Priority Order
1
Feed ten poor people
—
or
2
Clothe ten poor people
—
or
3
Free a slave
Freeing a slave is no longer practically applicable in this era
If unable
4
Fast three days
—

Ihram Violation Penalty (Fidyat al-Hajj)

Whoever commits a prohibited act during ihram (shaving, trimming nails, applying perfume, wearing sewn garments...) must offer a penalty.

Choice — the person may choose any option
1
Fast three days
—
or
2
Feed six poor people
—
or
3
Sacrifice a sheep
—

Hunting in Ihram Penalty

Whoever kills a land animal while in ihram must offer an equivalent sacrifice as judged by two just persons.

Choice — the person may choose any option
  • 1 Sacrifice the equivalent animal from livestock
  • 2 Or feed poor people the value of the equivalent
  • 3 Or fast one day for each poor person
The penalty varies depending on the animal killed and cannot be calculated here in general terms. Consult a scholar.
📊 Approximate amounts vary by country and local food prices
📋 This calculator is for guidance only and is not a fatwa — consult a scholar for your specific case
⚖️ One mudd is approximately 750 grams of the staple food of the region (rice or wheat)

📖 How to Use

Enter the cost of feeding one poor person (two meals or equivalent of one mudd of food), then select the type of kaffara from the tabs. The approximate cost will appear automatically along with the shari'a priority order of options.
